About

Some designers manage. Some still make things. Few do both.

I’ve spent 15+ years building teams, shaping brands, and never quite putting the tools down. That last part matters. At Vox Media, I took a three-person design group and built it into a 50-person department.

At Inman, I run design across the whole organization — product, events, editorial, brand. Smaller stage, sharper mandate.

Getting closer to execution opened a door I didn’t expect. I watched our dev team rebuild how they worked with AI — and decided to figure out how design could fit into that. The first version of everything needed rebuilding. That became the pattern — move fast, fail, fix — and build infrastructure we could actually rely on coming out the other side.

What I’ve learned is that working with AI isn’t a skill problem — it’s a collaboration problem. You problem-solve together, the same way you would with any good colleague.

I think in systems. I design for humans. I build teams that outperform their size, and I stay close enough to the work to know when something’s off.

Based in Lancaster, PA.
